how to style for react datepicker style hover end day

If the `.react-datepicker__day--end` class is not working in your React Datepicker, it's possible that you're using an older version of the package that doesn't support this feature.

The ability to style the end day of a selected date range was introduced in version 2.2.0 of the react-datepicker package, so you should make sure you're using at least that version or newer.

Another possible reason why the `.react-datepicker__day--end` class might not be working is that the styles are being overridden by other styles in your CSS. In this case, you can try using a more specific selector to apply the styles, such as `div.react-datepicker__day--end:hover`. You can also try adding the `!important` flag to your styles to make sure they take precedence over other styles.

If none of these solutions work, it's possible that there's a bug in your code. Make sure that you're using the correct props and options for the React Datepicker, and that there are no syntax errors or typos in your code. If you're still having trouble, you can try posting a question on the React Datepicker GitHub repository or Stack Overflow with more details about your issue.
